workflows.problem: US patients finish an AI-supported access assessment but still need a clear next step into available care.
workflows.hypothesis: A tighter routing handoff will lift completed-to-scheduled conversion while keeping safety review narrow and explicit.
workflows.okr: Improve access conversion, reduce avoidable manual work, and keep safety review measurable.

workflows.problem: Partner clinics need referrals routed toward provider panels that can actually accept the patient within the target window.
workflows.hypothesis: Capacity-aware matching will reduce dead-end referrals and shorten time to first available appointment.
workflows.okr: Improve access conversion, reduce avoidable manual work, and keep safety review measurable.

workflows.problem: US partner launches create repeat questions across consent language, escalation paths, routing rules, and analytics setup.
workflows.hypothesis: A productized launch checklist will compress onboarding time and prevent avoidable post-launch incidents.
workflows.okr: Improve access conversion, reduce avoidable manual work, and keep safety review measurable.

workflows.workflow: Referral status sync into partner EHR
workflows.problem: Manual status reconciliation makes it hard to know whether a referred patient was accepted, scheduled, or stuck.
workflows.hypothesis: A FHIR-backed status contract will reduce manual follow-up and make access outcomes measurable by partner.
workflows.okr: Improve access conversion, reduce avoidable manual work, and keep safety review measurable.

workflows.problem: High-risk language must move quickly from AI assessment to human review without swamping reviewers with weak signals.
workflows.hypothesis: A tuned escalation loop will preserve safety recall while reducing low-value manual review volume.
workflows.okr: Improve access conversion, reduce avoidable manual work, and keep safety review measurable.

workflows.problem: Coordinators need one place to accept, defer, or request more context for a referral.
workflows.hypothesis: A queue with status reasons and SLA prompts will improve acceptance speed and reduce support asks.
workflows.okr: Improve access conversion, reduce avoidable manual work, and keep safety review measurable.

workflows.problem: Patients who pause during assessment often do not understand whether their progress is saved or how to resume.
workflows.hypothesis: Recovery prompts can lift completion without adding pressure to a sensitive care-seeking moment.
workflows.okr: Improve access conversion, reduce avoidable manual work, and keep safety review measurable.

workflows.problem: Event naming drift blocks the team from reading the access funnel without analyst cleanup.
workflows.hypothesis: A governed taxonomy will let PMs and engineers answer funnel, cohort, and incident questions from the data.
workflows.okr: Improve access conversion, reduce avoidable manual work, and keep safety review measurable.

workflows.workflow: CS bug and scope intake triage
workflows.problem: Bugs, partner asks, implementation gaps, and one-off scope additions compete for the same engineering capacity.
workflows.hypothesis: A triage rubric will protect focus while still giving CS a transparent answer on every inbound item.
workflows.okr: Improve access conversion, reduce avoidable manual work, and keep safety review measurable.

instrumentation.product_question: What outcome closed the loop?
How quickly did the safety reviewer open the case?
Which match factors changed the recommended provider panel?
Which event contract was approved or rejected?
Which event properties are missing or malformed?
Did the clinic accept the referral within SLA?
What operational risk remains before go-live?
What route did the user see and did they continue?
Which patients complete assessment and reach a care-routing decision?
Where do users pause and which prompt can recover them?
What action did the clinic take on the referral?
Can PMs use the funnel without cleanup?
Was fresh capacity available when routing was decided?
Did recovery outreach bring the user back?
Did the recovered user complete intake?
Which launch gate slowed onboarding?
Which partner status updates are received from the EHR?
Which coordinator reviewed the queue?
Which work was protected from churn?
Was the request accepted, declined, merged, or routed?
Did the partner pass the QA checklist?
Which required fields fail mapping or validation?
Which high-risk phrases triggered review?
Did product and EHR status stay aligned?
What type of inbound request entered triage?
What evidence did the clinic need before acceptance?
Can we isolate intake starts by market, partner, and acquisition source?

briefs.project_overview: Improve digital self-referral intake routing for patient seeking care by converting the current ambiguity into a measured, owned workflow.
briefs.user_story: As a patient seeking care, I need uS patients finish an AI-supported access assessment but still need a clear next step into available care.
briefs.acceptance_criteria: Partner-facing status reason is visible and exportable.
briefs.non_goal: Do not build bespoke behavior for a single partner until signal repeats.
briefs.engineering_note: Timebox discovery and keep the sprint goal unchanged.

briefs.project_overview: Improve therapy referral capacity matching for clinic access lead by converting the current ambiguity into a measured, owned workflow.
briefs.user_story: As a clinic access lead, I need partner clinics need referrals routed toward provider panels that can actually accept the patient within the target window.
briefs.acceptance_criteria: Workflow has event coverage, owner, QA path, and customer-facing rationale.
briefs.non_goal: Do not add a new analytics surface before fixing the event contract.
briefs.engineering_note: Sequence schema migration before UI work.

briefs.project_overview: Improve us clinic launch readiness checklist for implementation manager by converting the current ambiguity into a measured, owned workflow.
briefs.user_story: As a implementation manager, I need uS partner launches create repeat questions across consent language, escalation paths, routing rules, and analytics setup.
briefs.acceptance_criteria: Incident and support handoff are documented before production rollout.
briefs.non_goal: Do not add a new analytics surface before fixing the event contract.
briefs.engineering_note: Sequence schema migration before UI work.

briefs.user_story: As a clinic operations admin, I need manual status reconciliation makes it hard to know whether a referred patient was accepted, scheduled, or stuck.
briefs.acceptance_criteria: PM, engineering, CS, and clinical owner agree on launch metric and rollback signal.
briefs.non_goal: Do not build bespoke behavior for a single partner until signal repeats.
briefs.engineering_note: Timebox discovery and keep the sprint goal unchanged.

briefs.project_overview: Improve crisis keyword escalation loop for clinical safety reviewer by converting the current ambiguity into a measured, owned workflow.
briefs.user_story: As a clinical safety reviewer, I need high-risk language must move quickly from AI assessment to human review without swamping reviewers with weak signals.
briefs.acceptance_criteria: Incident and support handoff are documented before production rollout.
briefs.non_goal: Do not automate clinical judgment beyond the agreed escalation policy.

briefs.acceptance_criteria: Workflow has event coverage, owner, QA path, and customer-facing rationale.
briefs.non_goal: Do not add a new analytics surface before fixing the event contract.

briefs.project_overview: Improve assessment completion recovery for patient who pauses intake by converting the current ambiguity into a measured, owned workflow.

briefs.non_goal: Do not automate clinical judgment beyond the agreed escalation policy.
